﻿The Project Gutenberg eBook of A wreath of cloud
Title: A wreath of cloud, Being the third part of 'The tale of Genji'
Author: Murasaki Shikibu
Translator:
pairs.quijote_source:
The
next day he went to dine with his friend, and was welcomed by Camilla,
who received and treated him with great cordiality, knowing the affection
her husband felt for him.

pairs.display: Worse than the general scandal and discredit, worse than the spectacle of Nyosan’s misery and entreaty, and repeated to him this sonnet on the fable or story of Pyramus and Thisbe.
pairs.genji_half: Worse than the general scandal and discredit, worse than the spectacle of Nyosan’s misery
pairs.quijote_half: and entreaty, and repeated to him this sonnet on the fable or story of Pyramus and Thisbe.
pairs.genji_source:
Worse than the general scandal and
discredit, worse than the spectacle of Nyosan’s misery and disgrace,
would be the knowledge that Genji no longer respected him.
pairs.quijote_source:
Don Lorenzo gave a proof of it, for he complied with Don
Quixote’s request and entreaty, and repeated to him this sonnet on the
fable or story of Pyramus and Thisbe.

pairs.display: Koremitsu ordered the man to pull in Genji’s carriage as quietly as he could For friend no longer is there friend;
pairs.genji_half: Koremitsu ordered the man to pull in Genji’s carriage as quietly as he could
pairs.quijote_half: For friend no longer is there friend;
pairs.genji_source:
Koremitsu ordered the man to pull in Genji’s carriage as
quietly as he could and himself went straight to the front door, which
he rattled, coughing as he did so that Shōnagon might know who was
there.
pairs.quijote_source:
This was why it was said--

For friend no longer is there friend;

pairs.display: Here Prince Genji claimed her as his daughter, thou art true to thy promises.
pairs.genji_half: Here Prince Genji claimed her as his daughter,
pairs.quijote_half: thou art true to thy promises.
pairs.genji_source:
Here Prince Genji claimed her as his daughter, Prince
Yūgiri as his sister....
pairs.quijote_source:
O giant Malambruno, though thou art an
enchanter, thou art true to thy promises.

pairs.display: How high-minded were Genji’s principles, That deals with life and death as with a play!
pairs.genji_half: How high-minded were Genji’s principles,
pairs.quijote_half: That deals with life and death as with a play!
pairs.genji_source:
How high-minded were Genji’s principles, and how
unsuccessful he was in applying them!
pairs.quijote_source:
Thus life doth slay,
And death again to life restoreth me;
  Strange destiny,
That deals with life and death as with a play!

pairs.display: Since it was utterly out of the question that Murasaki should pay the customary visit to the Princess’s quarters, love me well, Olalla;
pairs.genji_half: Since it was utterly out of the question that Murasaki should pay the customary visit to the Princess’s quarters,
pairs.quijote_half: love me well, Olalla;
pairs.genji_source:
Since it
was utterly out of the question that Murasaki should pay the customary
visit to the Princess’s quarters, the Princess, contrary to all
precedent, condescended to visit her.
pairs.quijote_source:
ANTONIO’S BALLAD

Thou dost love me well, Olalla;

pairs.display: Ukon had not seen her mistress at close quarters for some months past, Brings fortune to the cause that gave thee birth; Then banish sadness even in the tomb.
pairs.genji_half: Ukon had not seen her mistress at close quarters for some months past,
pairs.quijote_half: Brings fortune to the cause that gave thee birth; Then banish sadness even in the tomb.
pairs.genji_source:
Ukon had not seen her
mistress at close quarters for some months past, and could now have
sworn that even in that short space of time Lady Murasaki had grown
twice as handsome.
pairs.quijote_source:
Lay of despair, grieve not when thou art gone
Forth from this sorrowing heart: my misery
Brings fortune to the cause that gave thee birth;
Then banish sadness even in the tomb.

pairs.display: The younger among the gentlewomen-in-waiting had been placed at her disposal; while the older among them, ever looks askance? Chance.
pairs.genji_half: The younger among the gentlewomen-in-waiting had been placed at her disposal; while the older among them,
pairs.quijote_half: ever looks askance? Chance.
pairs.genji_source:
The younger among the
gentlewomen-in-waiting had been placed at her disposal; while the
older among them, and such as had distinguished themselves in any way,
were retained by Murasaki.
pairs.quijote_source:
What at my glory ever looks askance?
    Chance.

pairs.display: Fujitsubo’s son accordingly became Emperor under the title Ryōzen, and at a like racing speed made off and got clear of them all.
pairs.genji_half: Fujitsubo’s son accordingly became Emperor under the title Ryōzen,
pairs.quijote_half: and at a like racing speed made off and got clear of them all.
pairs.genji_source:
Fujitsubo’s son accordingly became Emperor under the title Ryōzen, and
Lady Jōkyōden’s little son became Crown Prince.
pairs.quijote_source:
There was no necessity for so many words or objurgations, for at the
first one Gines jumped down, and at a like racing speed made off and got
clear of them all.

pairs.display: But Genji was determined that this should not take place if there was any true, Andres my son?
pairs.genji_half: But Genji was determined that this should not take place if there was any
pairs.quijote_half: true, Andres my son?
pairs.genji_source:
But Genji was determined that this should not take place if there was
any chance that the girl’s reception there would in any quarter be
an unfavourable one.
pairs.quijote_source: Is not all this true, Andres my son?

pairs.display: I wish it were so; but though they are sisters and gall Of this vile age, mean and illiberal, That cannot even let a donkey be.
pairs.genji_half: I wish it were so; but though they are sisters
pairs.quijote_half: and gall Of this vile age, mean and illiberal, That cannot even let a donkey be.
pairs.genji_source:
I wish it were so; but though they are sisters and in some ways very
much alike, Ochiba is Ochiba and Nyosan is Nyosan.
pairs.quijote_source:
Within an ace of being Count was he,
    And would have been but for the spite and gall
    Of this vile age, mean and illiberal,
That cannot even let a donkey be.

pairs.display: She lived in apartments called Fujitsubo (Wistaria Tub) Don Cirongilio of Thrace?
pairs.genji_half: She lived in apartments called Fujitsubo (Wistaria Tub)
pairs.quijote_half: Don Cirongilio of Thrace?
pairs.genji_source:
She lived in apartments called Fujitsubo
(Wistaria Tub) and was known by this name.
pairs.quijote_source:
Who more impetuous than Don Cirongilio of
Thrace?

pairs.display: About this time Lady Fujitsubo fell ill of the Spanish nation!
pairs.genji_half: About this time Lady Fujitsubo fell ill
pairs.quijote_half: of the Spanish nation!
pairs.genji_source:
About this time Lady Fujitsubo fell ill and retired for a while from
the Palace.
pairs.quijote_source: O honour and mirror of the Spanish nation!

pairs.display: First he went to the Emperor, then the Heir Apparent and the Ex-Emperor, and arm of Mutius?
pairs.genji_half: First he went to the Emperor, then the Heir Apparent and the Ex-Emperor,
pairs.quijote_half: and arm of Mutius?
pairs.genji_source:
First he went to the
Emperor, then the Heir Apparent and the Ex-Emperor, and after that to
Princess Fujitsubo’s house in the Third Ward.
pairs.quijote_source: What burned the hand and arm of Mutius?

pairs.display: The matter was not one which he could discuss except with Fujitsubo herself, and cut off the gallant Spaniards under the command of the most courteous Cortes in the New World?
pairs.genji_half: The matter was not one which he could discuss except with Fujitsubo herself,
pairs.quijote_half: and cut off the gallant Spaniards under the command of the most courteous Cortes in the New World?
pairs.genji_source:
The matter was not one which he could discuss
except with Fujitsubo herself, and it was impossible to see her.
pairs.quijote_source:
And to come to more modern
examples, what scuttled the ships, and left stranded and cut off the
gallant Spaniards under the command of the most courteous Cortes in the
New World?

pairs.display: The best he could do was at any rate to strengthen Fujitsubo’s position city of El Toboso.
pairs.genji_half: The best he could do was at any rate to strengthen Fujitsubo’s position
pairs.quijote_half: city of El Toboso.
pairs.genji_source:
The best
he could do was at any rate to strengthen Fujitsubo’s position and
hope that later on she would be able to exert her influence.
pairs.quijote_source:
Why, in the great city of El
Toboso.

pairs.display: Both Fujitsubo and the Heir as it would be to pluck unripe the grapes of the fairest vineyard on its surface.
pairs.genji_half: Both Fujitsubo and the Heir
pairs.quijote_half: as it would be to pluck unripe the grapes of the fairest vineyard on its surface.
pairs.genji_source:
Both
Fujitsubo and the Heir Apparent were to be there.
pairs.quijote_source:
But that they have not, for Heaven will
not suffer so great a wrong to Earth, as it would be to pluck unripe the
grapes of the fairest vineyard on its surface.

pairs.display: Cautiously and stealthily he crept the pearls of the South, the gold of Tibar, and the balsam of Panchaia!
pairs.genji_half: Cautiously and stealthily he crept
pairs.quijote_half: the pearls of the South, the gold of Tibar, and the balsam of Panchaia!
pairs.genji_source:
Cautiously and
stealthily he crept towards Fujitsubo’s apartments and inspected them.
pairs.quijote_source:
And then when they promise the Phoenix of
Arabia, the crown of Ariadne, the horses of the Sun, the pearls of the
South, the gold of Tibar, and the balsam of Panchaia!

pairs.display: The dull sky marked (thought Fujitsubo) not only the gloom of the departing year, for my return to Spain to carry away the hidden treasure.
pairs.genji_half: The dull sky marked (thought Fujitsubo) not only the gloom of the departing year,
pairs.quijote_half: for my return to Spain to carry away the hidden treasure.
pairs.genji_source:
The
dull sky marked (thought Fujitsubo) not only the gloom of the departing
year, but the end of all fair prospects.
pairs.quijote_source:
He bade me go by all means, and said
that the next day we should discuss the plan to be adopted for my return
to Spain to carry away the hidden treasure.

pairs.display: In arriving at Fujitsubo’s rooms he sent in a message and when Zoraida entered it she said there were faces there like Lela Marien’s.
pairs.genji_half: In arriving at Fujitsubo’s rooms he sent in a message
pairs.quijote_half: and when Zoraida entered it she said there were faces there like Lela Marien’s.
pairs.genji_source:
In arriving at Fujitsubo’s rooms he sent in a message to explain that
he had been detained in the Presence.
pairs.quijote_source:
We went
straight to the church to return thanks to God for the mercies we had
received, and when Zoraida entered it she said there were faces there
like Lela Marien’s.

pairs.display: He must put Fujitsubo out of his thoughts; and wet through by the perpetual downpour of rainy snow, May thy joys be all dreams, And forgotten thy love.
pairs.genji_half: He must put Fujitsubo out of his thoughts; and wet through by the perpetual downpour of rainy snow,
pairs.quijote_half: May thy joys be all dreams, And forgotten thy love.
pairs.genji_source:
He must put Fujitsubo out of his thoughts; and wet
through by the perpetual downpour of rainy snow, he played his part in
the elaborate rituals and processions.
pairs.quijote_source:
May thy grandest adventures
  Discomfitures prove,
May thy joys be all dreams,
  And forgotten thy love.

pairs.display: The Service of the Eight Recitals was to be celebrated in Fujitsubo’s and beneficent disposal of heaven.
pairs.genji_half: The Service of the Eight Recitals was to be celebrated in Fujitsubo’s
pairs.quijote_half: and beneficent disposal of heaven.
pairs.genji_source:
The Service of the Eight Recitals was to be celebrated in Fujitsubo’s
house on the tenth of the twelfth month and the four succeeding
days.
pairs.quijote_source:
Quiteria belonged to Basilio and Basilio to Quiteria
by the just and beneficent disposal of heaven.

pairs.display: He was now firmly convinced that if it were not for that reason, and will keep his word about the promised island, in spite of the envy and malice of the world.
pairs.genji_half: He was now firmly convinced that if it were not
pairs.quijote_half: for that reason, and will keep his word about the promised island, in spite of the envy and malice of the world.
pairs.genji_source:
He was now firmly convinced that if it were not for his duty to
Fujitsubo’s son he would certainly retire from the world.
pairs.quijote_source:
My lord and
husband the duke, though not one of the errant sort, is none the less a
knight for that reason, and will keep his word about the promised island,
in spite of the envy and malice of the world.

pairs.display: The late Emperor had hoped that by investing Lady Fujitsubo and but for the barber being close by to catch her in his arms, she would have fallen completely to the ground.
pairs.genji_half: The late Emperor had hoped that by investing Lady Fujitsubo
pairs.quijote_half: and but for the barber being close by to catch her in his arms, she would have fallen completely to the ground.
pairs.genji_source:
The late
Emperor had hoped that by investing Lady Fujitsubo with definite
public rank he would assure the boy’s future.
pairs.quijote_source:
The instant she
recognised him, with a prolonged plaintive cry drawn from the depths of
her heart, she fell backwards fainting, and but for the barber being
close by to catch her in his arms, she would have fallen completely to
the ground.

pairs.display: He remembered that Fujitsubo would now have to provide he raised such an outcry that everyone in the inn came running to know what the noise and quarrel meant.
pairs.genji_half: He remembered that Fujitsubo would now have to provide
pairs.quijote_half: he raised such an outcry that everyone in the inn came running to know what the noise and quarrel meant.
pairs.genji_source:
He
remembered that Fujitsubo would now have to provide herself with such
articles as appertain to a nun’s life.
pairs.quijote_source:
The barber,
however, was not so ready to relinquish the prize he had made in the
pack-saddle; on the contrary, he raised such an outcry that everyone in
the inn came running to know what the noise and quarrel meant.

pairs.display: This was under the circumstances perfectly natural He slays, lays low, cleaves, hews; but art hath made A novel style for our new paladin.
pairs.genji_half: This was under the circumstances perfectly natural
pairs.quijote_half: He slays, lays low, cleaves, hews; but art hath made A novel style for our new paladin.
pairs.genji_source:
This was under the circumstances perfectly natural and Fujitsubo had
fully expected it.
pairs.quijote_source:
There hath he hung his arms and trenchant blade
Wherewith, achieving deeds till now unseen,
  He slays, lays low, cleaves, hews; but art hath made
A novel style for our new paladin.

pairs.display: So the old ladies whispered together, shaking their heads for he could see no satisfactory way out of this ill-advised business.
pairs.genji_half: So the old ladies whispered together, shaking their heads
pairs.quijote_half: for he could see no satisfactory way out of this ill-advised business.
pairs.genji_source:
So the old ladies whispered together, shaking their heads
and calling blessings upon him, while to Fujitsubo herself came many
painful recollections.
pairs.quijote_source:
Lothario
repaired to his own house, and Anselmo remained in his, as well satisfied
as Lothario was troubled in mind; for he could see no satisfactory way
out of this ill-advised business.

pairs.display: As the moon did not rise till after midnight he found himself with time on his hands, a plain proof that she had let herself down from it and escaped.
pairs.genji_half: As the moon did not rise till after midnight he found himself with time on his hands,
pairs.quijote_half: a plain proof that she had let herself down from it and escaped.
pairs.genji_source:
As the moon did not rise till
after midnight he found himself with time on his hands, and went first
to visit the Abbess Fujitsubo.
pairs.quijote_source:
He opened the door, entered, but found
no Leonela; all he found was some sheets knotted to the window, a plain
proof that she had let herself down from it and escaped.

pairs.display: To Fujitsubo he sent an account of the thunderstorm for an opportunity of making it known with safety to himself, being in dread of what his father the duke would do when he came to know of his folly.
pairs.genji_half: To Fujitsubo he sent an account of the thunderstorm
pairs.quijote_half: for an opportunity of making it known with safety to himself, being in dread of what his father the duke would do when he came to know of his folly.
pairs.genji_source:
To Fujitsubo he sent an account of the thunderstorm and
his own almost miraculous escape from harm.
pairs.quijote_source:
But, as afterwards appeared, when he said
this to me he had already enjoyed the peasant girl under the title of
husband, and was waiting for an opportunity of making it known with
safety to himself, being in dread of what his father the duke would do
when he came to know of his folly.

pairs.display: Under the circumstances he thought the best thing he could returned to their castle resolved to follow up their joke; for to them there was no reality that could afford them more amusement.
pairs.genji_half: Under the circumstances he thought the best thing he could
pairs.quijote_half: returned to their castle resolved to follow up their joke; for to them there was no reality that could afford them more amusement.
pairs.genji_source:
Under the circumstances he thought the best
thing he could do was to talk the matter over with Lady Fujitsubo.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke and
duchess, pleased with their hunt and at having carried out their plans so
cleverly and successfully, returned to their castle resolved to follow up
their joke; for to them there was no reality that could afford them more
amusement.

pairs.display: It soon became apparent that, in accordance with Fujitsubo’s advice, while the barber mounted his with a beard down to the waist of mingled red and white, for it was, as has been said, the tail of a clay-red ox.
pairs.genji_half: It soon became apparent that, in accordance with Fujitsubo’s advice,
pairs.quijote_half: while the barber mounted his with a beard down to the waist of mingled red and white, for it was, as has been said, the tail of a clay-red ox.
pairs.genji_source:
It soon became apparent that, in
accordance with Fujitsubo’s advice, he had decided to disregard the
retired Emperor’s wishes.
pairs.quijote_source:
He then put on his hat, which was broad enough to serve him for an
umbrella, and enveloping himself in his cloak seated himself
woman-fashion on his mule, while the barber mounted his with a beard down
to the waist of mingled red and white, for it was, as has been said, the
tail of a clay-red ox.

pairs.display: To one other person only and the language is polished and clear, studying and observing the style befitting the speaker with propriety and judgment.
pairs.genji_half: To one other person only
pairs.quijote_half: and the language is polished and clear, studying and observing the style befitting the speaker with propriety and judgment.
pairs.genji_source: To one other person only had he shown them—the ex-Empress Fujitsubo.
pairs.quijote_source:
All the adventures at the Castle of Miraguarda are
excellent and of admirable contrivance, and the language is polished and
clear, studying and observing the style befitting the speaker with
propriety and judgment.

pairs.display: The sliding-screen of the breakfast-room was now pushed aside where her husband was about to take passage for the Indies with an appointment of high honour.
pairs.genji_half: The sliding-screen of the breakfast-room was now pushed aside
pairs.quijote_half: where her husband was about to take passage for the Indies with an appointment of high honour.
pairs.genji_source:
The sliding-screen of the breakfast-room was now pushed aside and Lady
Fujitsubo entered.
pairs.quijote_source:
In the coach there was, as
afterwards appeared, a Biscay lady on her way to Seville, where her
husband was about to take passage for the Indies with an appointment of
high honour.

pairs.display: Lady Fujitsubo had fallen ill at the beginning of the year and pursued their journey, leaving the two adventurers a sorry sight and in sorrier mood.
pairs.genji_half: Lady Fujitsubo had fallen ill at the beginning of the year
pairs.quijote_half: and pursued their journey, leaving the two adventurers a sorry sight and in sorrier mood.
pairs.genji_source:
Lady Fujitsubo had fallen ill at the beginning of the year and since
the third month her malady had taken a serious turn.
pairs.quijote_source:
Then, seeing the mischief they had done, the Yanguesans with all the
haste they could loaded their team and pursued their journey, leaving the
two adventurers a sorry sight and in sorrier mood.

pairs.display: There was a certain priest who had while he attended to some pressing business, as he would return in an hour and a half.
pairs.genji_half: There was a certain priest who had
pairs.quijote_half: while he attended to some pressing business, as he would return in an hour and a half.
pairs.genji_source:
There was a certain priest who had for generations served as chaplain
in Lady Fujitsubo’s family.
pairs.quijote_source:
When dinner was over and the cloth removed,
Anselmo told Lothario to stay there with Camilla while he attended to
some pressing business, as he would return in an hour and a half.

pairs.display: In the spring of the next year the National Mourning for the most tender-hearted and faithful handmaid in the world, and her mistress for another persecuted Penelope.
pairs.genji_half: In the spring of the next year the National Mourning
pairs.quijote_half: for the most tender-hearted and faithful handmaid in the world, and her mistress for another persecuted Penelope.
pairs.genji_source:
In the spring of the next year the National Mourning for Lady
Fujitsubo came to an end.
pairs.quijote_source:
with more to the same effect, so that anyone who
heard her would have taken her for the most tender-hearted and faithful
handmaid in the world, and her mistress for another persecuted Penelope.

pairs.display: But he had another daughter, rose cager to learn what Leonela had to tell him, and hastened to the room where he had locked her in.
pairs.genji_half: But he had another daughter,
pairs.quijote_half: rose cager to learn what Leonela had to tell him, and hastened to the room where he had locked her in.
pairs.genji_source:
But
he had another daughter, born to him by a certain Princess Wistaria,
a younger sister of Lady Fujitsubo.
pairs.quijote_source:
As soon as daylight came Anselmo, without missing Camilla from his side,
rose cager to learn what Leonela had to tell him, and hastened to the
room where he had locked her in.

pairs.display: I hear the child is exceedingly good-looking, rather in Fujitsubo’s style, it was the opinion of most people that he would have been mad all the same.
pairs.genji_half: I hear the child is exceedingly good-looking, rather in Fujitsubo’s style,
pairs.quijote_half: it was the opinion of most people that he would have been mad all the same.
pairs.genji_source:
I hear the child is exceedingly
good-looking, rather in Fujitsubo’s style, they say; though of course
to a far less remarkable degree....

pairs.display: His relations with his father’s mistress, and from which she descended, not into the grave, but to become Queen of France; unless the histories lie?
pairs.genji_half: His relations with his father’s mistress,
pairs.quijote_half: and from which she descended, not into the grave, but to become Queen of France; unless the histories lie?
pairs.genji_source:
His relations with his father’s mistress, Fujitsubo.

 Sets of boxes fitting one into the other.
pairs.quijote_source:
Cowardly, spiritless
creature, art thou not in the very place the fair Magalona occupied, and
from which she descended, not into the grave, but to become Queen of
France; unless the histories lie?

pairs.display: But it was noticed with regret by his friends, farewell to the mock or imitation Arcadia, and more in humiliation than contentment, they continued their journey.
pairs.genji_half: But it was noticed with regret by his friends,
pairs.quijote_half: farewell to the mock or imitation Arcadia, and more in humiliation than contentment, they continued their journey.
pairs.genji_source:
But it was noticed
with regret by his friends, as a circumstance which must of necessity
be painful to him, that Murasaki still bore him no child.
pairs.quijote_source:
When they reached him master and man
mounted once more, and without going back to bid farewell to the mock or
imitation Arcadia, and more in humiliation than contentment, they
continued their journey.

pairs.display: During this time several Court festivals took place and Genji’s presence was constantly required; they were not held as captives, as we should set them free on the first opportunity.
pairs.genji_half: During this time several Court festivals took place and Genji’s presence was constantly required;
pairs.quijote_half: they were not held as captives, as we should set them free on the first opportunity.
pairs.genji_source:
During this time several Court
festivals took place and Genji’s presence was constantly required;
sometimes he was called upon to play the _koto_ or flute, sometimes to
serve his father in other ways.
pairs.quijote_source:
We gave the Moorish rowers
some food, and the renegade comforted them by telling them that they were
not held as captives, as we should set them free on the first
opportunity.

pairs.display: Certainly it was most unfortunate (the real situation being such as Tō no Chūjō guessed) Unblest is he That a bold front to Fortune dares not show, But soul and sense In bondage yieldeth up to indolence.
pairs.genji_half: Certainly it was most unfortunate (the real situation being such as Tō no Chūjō guessed)
pairs.quijote_half: Unblest is he That a bold front to Fortune dares not show, But soul and sense In bondage yieldeth up to indolence.
pairs.genji_source:
Certainly it was most unfortunate (the real situation
being such as Tō no Chūjō guessed) that Genji should have insisted upon
spreading this ridiculous report about the girl being his daughter.
pairs.quijote_source:
No victory,
No joy of triumph doth the faint heart know;
  Unblest is he
That a bold front to Fortune dares not show,
  But soul and sense
In bondage yieldeth up to indolence.

pairs.display: One came from the household of the Inspector, one from that of Tō no Chūjō’s step-brother Sayemon no Kami, who knows that the divine must not be forfeited for the sake of any human friendship?
pairs.genji_half: One came from the household of the Inspector, one from that of Tō no Chūjō’s step-brother Sayemon no Kami,
pairs.quijote_half: who knows that the divine must not be forfeited for the sake of any human friendship?
pairs.genji_source:
One came from the household of the Inspector, one from that
of Tō no Chūjō’s step-brother Sayemon no Kami, and one from Yoshikiyo,
who was now Governor of Ōmi.
pairs.quijote_source:
If
this, then, was a heathen’s feeling about friendship, how much more
should it be a Christian’s, who knows that the divine must not be
forfeited for the sake of any human friendship?

pairs.display: Yet it was from time to time clear that, despite his unchanging tenderness but he would not take any save five which Don Antonio lent him and he promised to repay at the capital.
pairs.genji_half: Yet it was from time to time clear that, despite his unchanging tenderness
pairs.quijote_half: but he would not take any save five which Don Antonio lent him and he promised to repay at the capital.
pairs.genji_source:
Yet it was from time to time clear
that, despite his unchanging tenderness and confidence, Murasaki was
not wholly contented with her present position.
pairs.quijote_source:
Ricote offered Don Gregorio a
thousand crowns if he would have them, but he would not take any save
five which Don Antonio lent him and he promised to repay at the capital.

pairs.display: But she had, after all, priority over her rival; and could not make out what had befallen him; and all his attendants were in the same state of amazement.
pairs.genji_half: But she had, after all, priority over her rival;
pairs.quijote_half: and could not make out what had befallen him; and all his attendants were in the same state of amazement.
pairs.genji_source:
But she
had, after all, priority over her rival; the Emperor had loved her
devotedly and she had born him princes.
pairs.quijote_source:
When the canon heard both the prisoner and the man who was at liberty
talk in such a strain he was ready to cross himself in his astonishment,
and could not make out what had befallen him; and all his attendants were
in the same state of amazement.

pairs.display: His gentlemen ran hither and thither, distracted; and replies he should show such excellent sense, and only lose his stirrups, as has been already said, when the subject of chivalry was broached.
pairs.genji_half: His gentlemen ran hither and thither, distracted;
pairs.quijote_half: and replies he should show such excellent sense, and only lose his stirrups, as has been already said, when the subject of chivalry was broached.
pairs.genji_source:
His gentlemen ran hither and thither, distracted; the Emperor’s envoys
thronged thick as the feet of the raindrops.
pairs.quijote_source:
The canon gazed at him, wondering at the extraordinary nature of his
madness, and that in all his remarks and replies he should show such
excellent sense, and only lose his stirrups, as has been already said,
when the subject of chivalry was broached.

pairs.display: Meanwhile Aoi’s possession had returned in full force; and secondly because it is said to have been written by a wise and witty king of Portugal.
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile Aoi’s possession had returned in full force;
pairs.quijote_half: and secondly because it is said to have been written by a wise and witty king of Portugal.
pairs.genji_source:
Meanwhile Aoi’s possession had returned in full force; she was in a
state of pitiable torment.
pairs.quijote_source:
This book,
gossip, is of authority for two reasons, first because it is very good,
and secondly because it is said to have been written by a wise and witty
king of Portugal.

pairs.display: The voice in which these words were said was not Aoi’s; though with shorter words, and without calling upon him to pay the reckoning let him go with a Godspeed.
pairs.genji_half: The voice in which these words were said was not Aoi’s;
pairs.quijote_half: though with shorter words, and without calling upon him to pay the reckoning let him go with a Godspeed.
pairs.genji_source:
The voice in which these words were
said was not Aoi’s; nor was the manner hers.
pairs.quijote_source:
The landlord, to get him out of the inn, replied with no less rhetoric
though with shorter words, and without calling upon him to pay the
reckoning let him go with a Godspeed.

pairs.display: He began trying to tell the Emperor something; lentils on Fridays, and a pigeon or so extra on Sundays, made away with three-quarters of his income.
pairs.genji_half: He began trying to tell the Emperor something;
pairs.quijote_half: lentils on Fridays, and a pigeon or so extra on Sundays, made away with three-quarters of his income.
pairs.genji_source:
He began trying to tell the Emperor something;
but he seemed unable to come to the point.
pairs.quijote_source:
An olla of rather more beef than mutton, a salad on most
nights, scraps on Saturdays, lentils on Fridays, and a pigeon or so extra
on Sundays, made away with three-quarters of his income.

pairs.display: A quiet time at home would not do her any harm; what art thou plotting, what art thou devising?
pairs.genji_half: A quiet time at home would not do her any harm;
pairs.quijote_half: what art thou plotting, what art thou devising?
pairs.genji_source:
A quiet time at home would not do her any harm; and although
she has been passed over on this occasion she really stands very well
with the Emperor.
pairs.quijote_source:
Unhappy, shortsighted Anselmo, what art thou doing, what art thou
plotting, what art thou devising?

pairs.display: They had been ordered to wear dove-grey lined with pale green; Noon but renews my wailing and my tears; And with the night again goes up my moan.
pairs.genji_half: They had been ordered to wear dove-grey lined with pale green;
pairs.quijote_half: Noon but renews my wailing and my tears; And with the night again goes up my moan.
pairs.genji_source:
They had been ordered to wear dove-grey lined with
pale green; the Emperor himself was to be dressed all in crimson.
pairs.quijote_source:
And when the sun ascends his star-girt throne,
  And on the earth pours down his midday beams,
    Noon but renews my wailing and my tears;
And with the night again goes up my moan.

pairs.display: On leave from the Palace; and bathed in her blood they were still uncertain as to the true nature of the act.
pairs.genji_half: On leave from the Palace;
pairs.quijote_half: and bathed in her blood they were still uncertain as to the true nature of the act.
pairs.genji_source: On leave from the Palace; she was one of the Emperor’s consorts.
pairs.quijote_source:
Leonela and Lothario stood amazed and astounded at the catastrophe, and
seeing Camilla stretched on the ground and bathed in her blood they were
still uncertain as to the true nature of the act.

pairs.display: I suppose you would have been wearing it at the Emperor’s Chrysanthemum Feast; And Love spreads wing from Cyprus isle to fly, A chink to view so wondrous great and small.
pairs.genji_half: I suppose you would have been wearing it at the Emperor’s Chrysanthemum Feast;
pairs.quijote_half: And Love spreads wing from Cyprus isle to fly, A chink to view so wondrous great and small.
pairs.genji_source:
I suppose you would have
been wearing it at the Emperor’s Chrysanthemum Feast; but of course
this odious hurricane has put a stop to everything of that sort.

pairs.display: Here the really smart equipages were seen in greatest abundance; and as she did so she said to him in a low voice.
pairs.genji_half: Here the really smart equipages were seen in greatest abundance;
pairs.quijote_half: and as she did so she said to him in a low voice.
pairs.genji_source:
Here the
really smart equipages were seen in greatest abundance; among them,
that of Lady Tamakatsura.
pairs.quijote_source:
They sent for some oil of John’s wort, and Altisidora herself
with her own fair hands bandaged all the wounded parts; and as she did so
she said to him in a low voice.

pairs.display: Nothing would be gained if I restored her to her father; and to protect her father against the kinsmen of Don Vicente and all the world, should they seek to injure him.
pairs.genji_half: Nothing would be gained if I restored her to her father;
pairs.quijote_half: and to protect her father against the kinsmen of Don Vicente and all the world, should they seek to injure him.
pairs.genji_source:
Nothing would be gained if I restored her to her father; for he too
has already supplied His Majesty with a consort.
pairs.quijote_source:
He
applauded her pious resolution, and offered to accompany her
whithersoever she wished, and to protect her father against the kinsmen
of Don Vicente and all the world, should they seek to injure him.

pairs.display: They now begged for a drive; mounted on two dromedaries, for not less tall were the two mules they rode on.
pairs.genji_half: They now begged for a drive;
pairs.quijote_half: mounted on two dromedaries, for not less tall were the two mules they rode on.
pairs.genji_source:
They now begged for a drive; but as he was going straight to
Tamakatsura’s, this was rather embarrassing.
pairs.quijote_source:
While they were thus talking there appeared on the road two friars of the
order of St. Benedict, mounted on two dromedaries, for not less tall were
the two mules they rode on.

pairs.display: She was now Consort of the Emperor; one morning before the dawning of the day (which was one of the hottest of the month of July)
pairs.genji_half: She was now Consort of the Emperor;
pairs.quijote_half: one morning before the dawning of the day (which was one of the hottest of the month of July)
pairs.genji_source:
She was now Consort of the Emperor; but had not been proclaimed
Empress.
pairs.quijote_source:
So, without giving notice of his intention to anyone, and
without anybody seeing him, one morning before the dawning of the day
(which was one of the hottest of the month of July)

pairs.display: Meanwhile the servants of Yūgao’s house were surprised that they had had no news from her and drums once more, from which they concluded that the Distressed Duenna was making her entrance.
pairs.genji_half: Meanwhile the servants of Yūgao’s house were surprised that they had had no news from her
pairs.quijote_half: and drums once more, from which they concluded that the Distressed Duenna was making her entrance.
pairs.genji_source:
Meanwhile the servants of
Yūgao’s house were surprised that they had had no news from her nor
even from Ukon, and had begun to be seriously disquieted.
pairs.quijote_source:
They would have carried on this duenna dispute further had they not heard
the notes of the fife and drums once more, from which they concluded that
the Distressed Duenna was making her entrance.

pairs.display: The priests carrying the sacred image arrived somewhat late, and it was evening and discover what kind of madness his was, Vivaldo proceeded to ask him what knights-errant meant.
pairs.genji_half: The priests carrying the sacred image arrived somewhat late, and it was evening
pairs.quijote_half: and discover what kind of madness his was, Vivaldo proceeded to ask him what knights-errant meant.
pairs.genji_source:
The priests carrying
the sacred image arrived somewhat late, and it was evening when the
little girls sent from the various quarters of Genji’s household
arrived with their thank-offerings and alms.
pairs.quijote_source:
The instant they heard this all set him down as mad, and the better to
settle the point and discover what kind of madness his was, Vivaldo
proceeded to ask him what knights-errant meant.

pairs.display: Though she has never been mentioned before, availing himself of the artifice of Hercules when he strangled Antaeus the son of Terra in his arms.
pairs.genji_half: Though she has never been mentioned before,
pairs.quijote_half: availing himself of the artifice of Hercules when he strangled Antaeus the son of Terra in his arms.
pairs.genji_source:
Though she has never been mentioned before,
Murasaki speaks of her as though the reader already knew all about
her.
pairs.quijote_source:
He thought more
of Bernardo del Carpio because at Roncesvalles he slew Roland in spite of
enchantments, availing himself of the artifice of Hercules when he
strangled Antaeus the son of Terra in his arms.

pairs.display: Sometimes the idea came to him that if he simply went straight to Kumoi’s father not transparent ones like Trifaldin’s, but so close that they allowed nothing to be seen through them.
pairs.genji_half: Sometimes the idea came to him that if he simply went straight to Kumoi’s father
pairs.quijote_half: not transparent ones like Trifaldin’s, but so close that they allowed nothing to be seen through them.
pairs.genji_source:
Sometimes the idea came to him that if he simply went straight to
Kumoi’s father and tackled him about the matter—insisted, shouted, made
a great scene—Tō no Chūjō would suddenly give in.
pairs.quijote_source:
The twelve duennas and the lady came on at procession pace, their faces
being covered with black veils, not transparent ones like Trifaldin’s,
but so close that they allowed nothing to be seen through them.

pairs.display: So riding upon a strange horse and wearing a hunting-cloak, and was answered that they were going to take ship for Rome, and that between them they might have about sixty reals.
pairs.genji_half: So riding upon a strange horse and wearing a hunting-cloak,
pairs.quijote_half: and was answered that they were going to take ship for Rome, and that between them they might have about sixty reals.
pairs.genji_source:
So riding upon a strange horse and wearing a
hunting-cloak, he had got himself up altogether so villainously
that he was able to follow Genji without being recognized upon the
road.
pairs.quijote_source:
Roque asked the pilgrims the same questions he had put to the captains,
and was answered that they were going to take ship for Rome, and that
between them they might have about sixty reals.

pairs.display: She was thinking with pleasure that the setting moon would light them on their way, and roguishly; but the fair Dido was shown dropping tears the size of walnuts from her eyes.
pairs.genji_half: She was thinking with pleasure that the setting moon would light them on their way,
pairs.quijote_half: and roguishly; but the fair Dido was shown dropping tears the size of walnuts from her eyes.
pairs.genji_source:
She was thinking with pleasure that the setting moon would light them
on their way, and Genji was just saying so when suddenly the moon
disappeared behind a bank of clouds.
pairs.quijote_source:
He noticed in the two stories that Helen did
not go very reluctantly, for she was laughing slyly and roguishly; but
the fair Dido was shown dropping tears the size of walnuts from her eyes.

pairs.display: Ukon who knew that they would assail her with questions which her promise to Genji forbade her to answer, steed that looks like a grey ass, which that Martino that your worship overthrew has left deserted here?
pairs.genji_half: Ukon who knew that they would assail her with questions which her promise to Genji forbade her to answer,
pairs.quijote_half: steed that looks like a grey ass, which that Martino that your worship overthrew has left deserted here?
pairs.genji_source:
Ukon who knew that they would assail her with questions which her
promise to Genji forbade her to answer, dared not go to the house, not
even to get news of her lady’s child.
pairs.quijote_source:
But putting that
aside, will your worship tell me what are we to do with this dapple-grey
steed that looks like a grey ass, which that Martino that your worship
overthrew has left deserted here?

pairs.display: Ki no Kami was surprised but by no means ill-pleased, and surrounded him, urging him to return and console his father at once and without a moment’s delay.
pairs.genji_half: Ki no Kami was surprised but by no means ill-pleased,
pairs.quijote_half: and surrounded him, urging him to return and console his father at once and without a moment’s delay.
pairs.genji_source:
Ki no Kami was surprised but by no means
ill-pleased, for he attributed Genji’s visit to the amenity of the
little pools and fountains which he had constructed in his garden.
pairs.quijote_source:
All the four who
had come in quest of Don Luis had now come into the inn and surrounded
him, urging him to return and console his father at once and without a
moment’s delay.
